title: Tooltip
subtitle: Informational tooltips with support for text and custom templates

basic.title: Basic tooltips
basic.description: Shows simple tooltips with text on hover.
basic.note: You can hide the arrow with nuiTooltipShowArrow.

positions.title: Positions
positions.description: Control the tooltip position relative to the element: top (default), bottom, left, right.

events.title: Events
events.description: Define what event triggers the tooltip: hover (default), click, focus, or manual for programmatic control.

delays.title: Delays
delays.description: Configure the delays before showing and hiding the tooltip.
delays.note: Use the nuiTooltipShowDelay and nuiTooltipHideDelay properties to adjust the waiting times.

templates.title: Custom templates
templates.description: Use Angular templates to display rich HTML content in the tooltip.

interactive.title: Interactive tooltips
interactive.description: Allows interacting with tooltip content. Useful for including buttons or links.
interactive.note: Enable the nuiTooltipInteractive property to allow interaction inside the tooltip without it closing automatically when the cursor is moved.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Dynamically control whether the tooltip is enabled or disabled.
disabled.note: Use the nuiTooltipDisabled property to enable or disable the tooltip as needed.

usage.title: Usage examples
usage.description: Complete usage examples of the nuiTooltip directive in Angular components

container.title: Tooltip container
container.description: CSS variables to control the appearance of the tooltip container: colors, dimensions, padding, font, borders, shadow and z-index

arrow.title: Tooltip arrow
arrow.description: CSS variable to control the size of the tooltip arrow

animation.title: Animations
animation.description: CSS variables to control the duration and timing of tooltip enter/exit animations

examples.title: Customization examples
examples.description: SCSS examples to customize tooltip styles in your application

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: Tooltips follow the WAI-ARIA Tooltip pattern, associating the floating content with its trigger via aria-describedby.

ariaDescribedby.description: The trigger element receives aria-describedby pointing to the tooltip's id, so screen readers announce the tooltip text as supplemental description.

tooltipRole.description: The floating tooltip carries role="tooltip" and a unique id referenced by aria-describedby.

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The trigger element must have its own accessible name independent of the tooltip. The tooltip only provides a supplemental description.
naming.note: Do not rely on the tooltip as the sole source of accessible name for triggers that only show an icon. Use aria-label on the trigger element.

trigger.description: The trigger must have its own accessible name (button text, aria-label…) that is meaningful without the tooltip text.

iconTrigger.description: For triggers that only show an icon, add an explicit aria-label on the trigger element.

tooltipContent.description: The tooltip text is connected via aria-describedby and serves as supplemental information, not as the main label.

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: Tooltips show and hide automatically based on focus. No explicit key press is required to open them.

tab.description: Tabbing to a trigger element shows the tooltip. Tabbing away hides it.

escape.description: Pressing Escape while the trigger has focus hides the tooltip.

setup.title: Global configuration
setup.description: Set default values for all Tooltip instances in your application using the provideNUI() function. Any property defined here is automatically applied to every tooltip that does not override it via a direct input binding.
setup.note: Only the properties you want to override need to be defined. Properties not specified fall back to the library defaults.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: Full list of configurable properties through TooltipConfig, their types and the library defaults.

rows.position: Preferred position of the tooltip relative to the trigger element
rows.event: Interaction type that triggers the tooltip to show
rows.showDelay: Delay in milliseconds before the tooltip becomes visible after triggering
rows.hideDelay: Delay in milliseconds before the tooltip hides after the trigger condition ends
rows.showArrow: Whether a directional arrow pointing to the trigger element is displayed
rows.allowHtml: Whether the tooltip content is rendered as HTML
rows.interactive: Whether the tooltip remains visible when the cursor moves over it

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: Values are resolved following a strict three-level priority chain. The first matching level wins: direct input > global config > library default.
priority.note: The priority chain guarantees that components always have a valid resolved value. A direct input always wins; if not provided, the global config value is used; if not configured either, the library default is applied.
